---
title: "LeetCode 155. Min Stack"
subtitle: "最小堆疊"
lang: zh-TW
date: 2026-07-16
author: Anthony
categories: [LeetCode, Array]
description: "[題目連結](https://leetcode.com/problems/min-stack/description/)"
image: ../../cover/Leetcode.svg
alternate-lang: "/dsa/posts/leetcode/0155-minstack/0155-minstack-en.html"
---

這個題目要求設計一個除了基本的 `push`（入堆）、`pop`（出堆）與 `top`（查頂）外，還能在 $\mathcal{O}(1)$ 內取得堆疊中最小值的資料結構。

## 想法

一開始題目沒有看仔細，很開心地寫了以下的 code：

```python
class MinStack:

    def __init__(self):
        self._stack = []        

    def push(self, value: int) -> None:
        self._stack.append(value)

    def pop(self) -> None:
        self._stack.pop()

    def top(self) -> int:
        if not self._stack:
            return 0
        return self._stack[-1]

    def getMin(self) -> int:
        return min(self._stack)
```

並且開心地按下執行。接著：[**Time Limit Exceeded**]{style="color: red;"}。

![](images/argh.gif){fig-align="center"}

細看題目，它需要每個函數都能在常數時間運行。

前幾個函數都不難，因為堆疊的入堆、出堆、查頂都是 $\mathcal{O}(1)$。比較複雜的是取得堆疊內的最小值。堆疊在查詢的操作上需要遍歷整個堆疊，才可以找到資料，到這邊似乎就卡住了。

不過！既然這是一個堆疊，何不直接使用其特性，也就是入、出堆與查頂都是常數時間呢？只要每次將元素推入堆疊中時，比較該元素與目前已知最小值的大小，並將較小的那一個同步推入另一個輔助堆疊中，即可讓這個輔助堆疊的堆頂，永遠代表**目前主堆疊中的最小值**，因此可以取該堆疊名稱為 `minStack`！（題目的深意在此啊...）

換句話說，輔助堆疊的每一層，都保存了入堆時的最小值。兩個堆疊的長度永遠保持一致且一一對應。如此一來，上述四個操作就全部達到常數時間，代價是多使用了一個與主堆疊等長的輔助空間，屬於典型的以空間換取時間。

## 實作

::: {.panel-tabset}
## C

```c
typedef struct
{
    int *_stack;
    int *_minStack; // shadow stack: running minimum at each depth
    int count;
} MinStack;

MinStack *minStackCreate()
{
    MinStack *obj = (MinStack *)malloc(sizeof(MinStack));
    obj->count = 0;
    obj->_stack = (int *)malloc(30000 * sizeof(int));
    obj->_minStack = (int *)malloc(30000 * sizeof(int));
    return obj;
}

void minStackPush(MinStack *obj, int value)
{
    obj->_stack[obj->count] = value;

    // Push the smaller of the new value and the current minimum
    if (obj->count == 0)
        obj->_minStack[obj->count] = value;
    else
        obj->_minStack[obj->count] = value < obj->_minStack[obj->count - 1] ? value : obj->_minStack[obj->count - 1];

    obj->count++;
}

void minStackPop(MinStack *obj)
{
    // Both stacks shrink together
    obj->count--;
}

int minStackTop(MinStack *obj)
{
    return obj->_stack[obj->count - 1];
}

int minStackGetMin(MinStack *obj)
{
    return obj->_minStack[obj->count - 1];
}

void minStackFree(MinStack *obj)
{
    free(obj->_stack);
    free(obj->_minStack);
    free(obj);
}
```

## C++

```cpp
class MinStack
{
private:
    vector<int> _stack;
    vector<int> _minStack; // shadow stack: running minimum at each depth

public:
    MinStack()
    {
    }

    void push(int value)
    {
        _stack.push_back(value);

        // Push the smaller of the new value and the current minimum
        if (_minStack.empty())
            _minStack.push_back(value);
        else
        {
            int minValue = min(value, _minStack.back());
            _minStack.push_back(minValue);
        }
    }

    void pop()
    {
        // Both stacks shrink together
        _stack.pop_back();
        _minStack.pop_back();
    }

    int top()
    {
        return _stack.back();
    }

    int getMin()
    {
        return _minStack.back();
    }
};
```

## Python

```python
class MinStack:

    def __init__(self):
        self._stack = []
        self._minStack = []  # shadow stack: running minimum at each depth

    def push(self, value: int) -> None:
        self._stack.append(value)

        # Push the smaller of the new value and the current minimum
        if not self._minStack:
            self._minStack.append(value)
            return
        min_value = min(value, self._minStack[-1])
        self._minStack.append(min_value)
        return

    def pop(self) -> None:
        # Both stacks shrink together
        self._stack.pop()
        self._minStack.pop()

    def top(self) -> int:
        if not self._stack:
            return 0
        return self._stack[-1]

    def getMin(self) -> int:
        return self._minStack[-1]
```
:::
